question: Is the image presented in a strict top-down flat lay perspective (camera perpendicular to the table)?
Is the background a dark wood table with visible wood grain representing the oceans (negative space)?
Are the continental outlines of the world map clearly traced in a white granular substance resembling sea salt?
Are the major continental shapes (North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, Australia) recognizable?
Are there exactly eight distinct piles of ingredients visible in the image?
Is each ingredient pile distinct and separated without mixing into other piles?
Do the Coffee Beans appear as roasted, oval beans with a central crease?
Are the Loose Tea Leaves depicted as dried, curled, or twisted leaf fragments?
Is the Tea Leaves pile placed in East Asia (China) or South Asia (India)?
Are the Black Peppercorns depicted as small, round, dark wrinkled spheres?
Is the Black Peppercorn pile placed in South Asia (India/Malabar region)?
Are the True Cinnamon Sticks depicted as thin, multi-layered rolled bark quills?
Is the Cinnamon Stick pile placed near Sri Lanka or South India?
Do the Clove Buds have a characteristic nail-like shape with a bulbous head?
Is the Clove Buds pile placed in Southeast Asia (Indonesia)?
Is the Nutmeg depicted as oval, brown, textured seeds/nuts?
Is the Nutmeg pile placed in Southeast Asia (Indonesia)?
Are the Vanilla Pods depicted as long, thin, dark brown/black bean pods?
Is the Quinoa depicted as small, round, pale/cream-colored grains?
Is the Quinoa pile placed in the Andes region of South America (Peru/Bolivia)?
Is the image rendered in a high-detail photorealistic style?
Is the lighting natural and soft, without harsh shadows?
